description: Architectural change: resolve repo: operations inline at their declaration position in the sequential pass, not eagerly in Phase 1. Sub-repos run their own pipeline in isolation; their sub-composite + deferred auto-merges integrate into the parent composite at the repo: op's position. This fully fixes the reported bug.
depends_on: context/sequential-operations.json (Phase A must be merged first — sequential consumer ops infrastructure is a prerequisite)

architecture.approach: Eager clone, lazy resolve. Phase 1 still discovers and clones all repos up-front. Phase 4 becomes a sequential pass over the consumer's operations. When a repo: op fires, the already-cloned sub-repo's processing (Phase 2) and composition (Phase 4) run on-demand, producing a sub-composite that integrates into the parent composite at that point.

key_files.orchestrator: src/phases/orchestrator.rs — drives the pipeline; needs lazy repo: resolution
key_files.discovery: src/phases/discovery.rs — Phase 1; stays eager (clone all up-front)
key_files.processing: src/phases/processing.rs — Phase 2; already per-repo; needs to be callable on-demand
key_files.composite: src/phases/composite.rs — Phase 4; needs incremental composition support
key_files.local_merge: src/phases/local_merge.rs — Phase 5; mostly handled by Phase A; minor coordination
key_files.config: src/config.rs:987 — Operation enum; already has Repo variant

session_startup: Run git status - verify on feat/sequential-operations-* branch
Verify Phase A is archived (context/completed/sequential-operations.json exists)
Read this plan file, find first task where status=pending and blocked_by=null

tasks.name: Read final spec rules and current code to understand the gap
tasks.description: Build a mental model of the current Phase 1-4 code path vs the spec's sequential model. Identify exactly which functions need to change.
steps: Read spec/detailed/operators.allium (ApplyRepoInline rule)
Read spec/detailed/auto-merge-composition.allium (DeferredMergeAtDeclarationPosition)
Read src/phases/orchestrator.rs — understand how phases are driven today
Read src/phases/discovery.rs — understand how repo: ops trigger Phase 1 discovery
Read src/phases/composite.rs — understand how IntermediateFSs are folded
Read src/phases/processing.rs:212-268 — understand apply_operation and how repo: is handled
Write a gap analysis: for each spec rule, which code path implements it today (if any), and what needs to change
acceptance_criteria: Agent can describe the current Phase 1-4 code path for repo: resolution
Agent has a gap analysis: spec rule → current code → needed change

tasks.name: Write E2E test reproducing the concrete bug scenario
tasks.description: E2E test that reproduces the exact scenario from context/prompt.md. This is the load-bearing acceptance test for Phase B.
steps: Create tests/cli_e2e_repo_in_sequence.rs
Set up upstream repo with: include src/**, rename src/(.*)$ → $1, yaml auto-merge .pre-commit-config.yaml with array_mode append_unique
Set up consumer with self: block: include src/** → template → template-vars → rename src/(.*)$ → $1 → repo: <upstream>
Consumer has src/.pre-commit-config.yaml with local hooks content
Assert: final .pre-commit-config.yaml contains BOTH local and upstream hooks
Run: cargo nextest run --test cli_e2e_repo_in_sequence
Expected: FAIL (current code resolves repo: eagerly, auto-merge fires before consumer ops)
acceptance_criteria: E2E test exists and FAILS with current code
Test exercises the full self: block scenario from the bug report
Test will PASS after repo-in-sequence is implemented

tasks.name: Fix load_local_fs to not skip dotfiles
tasks.description: load_local_fs in local_merge.rs:124 blanket-skips all files and directories starting with '.'. This breaks self: blocks that need to include local dotfiles (e.g., .pre-commit-config.yaml, .eslintrc.yaml, .editorconfig). The spec (MergeLocalFiles rule) lists specific directories to skip (.git, .svn, node_modules, etc.) but does NOT say skip all dotfiles. The code should skip only the specific directories listed in SKIP_DIRS, not all hidden files. Upstream dotfiles work fine (they flow through the composite, not load_local_fs). Failing E2E tests already exist in tests/cli_e2e_dotfiles.rs.
steps: Read src/phases/local_merge.rs:79-158 (load_local_fs function)
Read spec/common-repo.allium MergeLocalFiles rule for the authoritative skip list
Remove the blanket file_name.starts_with('.') skip at line 124
Keep the specific SKIP_DIRS list (.git, .svn, .hg, etc.) — these are still correct
Keep the .common-repo.yaml / .commonrepo.yaml config file skip
Write a unit test: load_local_fs loads dotfiles from a temp dir
Run: cargo nextest run --test cli_e2e_dotfiles --features integration-tests (both tests should pass)
Run: cargo nextest run (all existing tests still pass)
acceptance_criteria: load_local_fs loads dotfiles from the working directory
Specific dirs (.git, node_modules, etc.) are still skipped
Config files (.common-repo.yaml) are still skipped
cli_e2e_dotfiles tests pass
All existing tests pass

tasks.name: Separate cloning from resolution in Phase 1
tasks.description: Phase 1 currently discovers, clones, and processes repos in one pass. Separate cloning (which stays eager) from processing/resolution (which becomes lazy). The cloned repo data should be available as a lookup for the sequential pass to consume on-demand.
steps: Read src/phases/discovery.rs fully
Identify the boundary between 'clone repo' and 'parse and process repo config'
Extract a ClonedRepo struct or equivalent that holds the cloned working tree path + raw config but has NOT been processed into an IntermediateFS
Phase 1 produces a Map<RepoKey, ClonedRepo> instead of (or in addition to) the current RepoTree of processed nodes
Existing Phase 2/3/4 code continues to work (backward compatible)
Write unit tests for the new extraction
Run: cargo nextest run
acceptance_criteria: Clone and resolve are separable
Phase 1 can produce cloned-but-unprocessed repo data
Existing pipeline still works (no behavioral change yet)
All tests pass

tasks.name: Add resolve_repo_inline to orchestrator
tasks.description: Add a function that, given a ClonedRepo (or equivalent), runs the sub-repo's full processing pipeline (Phase 2-4) on demand and returns a sub-composite + deferred merge ops. This is the core of the lazy resolution mechanism.
steps: Add resolve_repo_inline(cloned_repo, parent_composite) -> (SubComposite, Vec<DeferredOp>) to orchestrator
Internally: parse sub-repo config, run Phase 2 processing, handle sub-repo's own repo: ops recursively, build sub-composite
The sub-repo's pipeline runs in isolation (its own MemoryFS, not the parent's)
Return the sub-composite and collected deferred merge ops
Write unit tests: resolve a simple repo with known files, verify sub-composite content
Run: cargo nextest run
acceptance_criteria: resolve_repo_inline function exists and is tested
Sub-repo pipeline runs in isolation (does not see parent composite)
Recursive repo: resolution works (sub-repo's own repo: ops fire within its pipeline)
All tests pass

tasks.name: Support incremental composite construction
tasks.description: The current Phase 4 composite builder folds all IntermediateFSs in one pass. Add support for incremental additions: integrate a sub-composite into an existing composite at a specific point, including auto-merge conflict resolution.
steps: Read src/phases/composite.rs fully
Add integrate_sub_composite(parent_composite, sub_composite, deferred_ops) that merges a sub-composite into the parent
Handle auto-merge conflicts inline (reuse existing auto-merge logic from AutoMergeInComposite)
Execute applicable deferred merges against the parent composite at this point (DeferredMergeAtDeclarationPosition)
Deferred merges whose dest is not yet in parent composite are returned for later Phase 5 execution
Write unit tests for incremental integration
Run: cargo nextest run
acceptance_criteria: integrate_sub_composite function exists and is tested
Auto-merge conflicts handled correctly
Deferred merges fire against parent composite where dest exists
Deferred merges for missing dests are returned for Phase 5
All tests pass

tasks.name: Wire up sequential operations pass in Phase 4
tasks.description: Connect the pieces: the orchestrator's Phase 4 now walks the consumer's operations in declaration order. Filter ops modify the composite directly. Repo: ops call resolve_repo_inline and integrate_sub_composite. This is the main integration point.
steps: Modify Phase 4 in orchestrator to iterate consumer ops in declaration order
For filter ops (include/exclude/rename/template/template-vars): apply to composite using existing operator implementations
For repo: ops: call resolve_repo_inline, then integrate_sub_composite
For merge ops: apply merge against composite
Collect residual deferred merges (dests not in composite) for Phase 5
Update Phase 5 to receive and execute residual deferred merges
Run: cargo nextest run
Run: cargo nextest run --test cli_e2e_repo_in_sequence
Expected: E2E test now PASSES
acceptance_criteria: Phase 4 is a sequential pass over consumer ops
Repo: ops resolve inline at their declaration position
The failing E2E test from write-failing-e2e-test now PASSES
All existing tests still pass

tasks.name: Update self: pipeline to use sequential Phase 4
tasks.description: The self: pipeline runs its own Phase 1-6. Update it to use the new sequential Phase 4. Self blocks pull local files via include operators, so the self pipeline's Phase 5 combine-with-local step may simplify.
steps: Read ExecuteSelfPipeline logic in orchestrator
Update self pipeline invocation to use the sequential Phase 4 pass
Verify include in self block sources from local working directory
Verify repo: in self block resolves inline (the bug scenario)
Run E2E tests including the bug scenario test
Run: cargo nextest run
acceptance_criteria: Self pipeline uses sequential Phase 4
Bug scenario E2E test passes
All tests pass

tasks.name: Add sequential-execution context hints to error messages
tasks.description: The spec (spec/detailed/merge-operations.allium) calls for error messages to include hints like 'Was the source possibly renamed or excluded by a preceding operation?' when a merge source is missing. The current implementation produces bare missing-source errors without sequential-execution context. This touches all merge format modules.
steps: Read spec/detailed/merge-operations.allium for the exact diagnostic hint guidance (MissingSourceExplicit, MissingSourceAutoMerge rules)
Read src/merge/json.rs, src/merge/yaml.rs, src/merge/toml.rs, src/merge/ini.rs, src/merge/markdown.rs, src/merge/xml.rs to find missing-source error paths
Determine how to thread sequential-execution context (e.g. list of preceding operations) to the error site
Add hint text to missing-source errors: suggest the source may have been renamed or excluded by a preceding operation
Write unit tests: trigger a missing-source error and assert the hint text is present
Run: cargo nextest run
Run: cargo xtask check-prose src/merge/
acceptance_criteria: Missing-source errors in all 6 merge modules include a sequential-context hint
Hint text matches the spec guidance from merge-operations.allium
Unit tests verify hint text presence
No AI writing pattern violations in error messages
All tests pass

tasks.name: Add phased implementation annotations to spec files
tasks.description: The spec files describe behavior that goes beyond what is currently implemented (e.g., ApplyRepoInline lazy resolution is Phase B, include-from-source-FS is Phase B, rename chaining is Phase B). The spec needs annotations distinguishing 'implemented now' from 'planned future' so readers can tell what the code actually does vs. what is aspirational. This is a documentation/spec task with no code changes.
steps: Read spec/common-repo.allium, spec/detailed/operators.allium, spec/detailed/auto-merge-composition.allium, spec/detailed/merge-operations.allium
For each rule, determine whether the behavior is implemented (Phase A), planned (Phase B), or future/unscheduled
Add annotations (e.g., comments or metadata fields) to each rule indicating implementation status: [Implemented: Phase A], [Planned: Phase B], [Future]
Use a consistent annotation format that does not break the Allium parser
Review annotations for accuracy against current code and Phase B plan
Commit: 'docs(spec): add phased implementation status annotations'
acceptance_criteria: All spec rules have implementation-phase annotations
Annotations are consistent in format across all spec files
Annotations accurately reflect current implementation state
Allium spec files remain parseable (annotations do not break syntax)

tasks.name: Fix rename::apply to chain mappings per RenameEquivalence spec invariant
tasks.description: The spec (RenameEquivalence in spec/detailed/operators.allium) says mappings within a single rename op should chain: the first mapping's output feeds the second mapping's input. But src/operators.rs rename::apply captures current_files once at the start and does not chain — each mapping operates on the original file set independently. This was noted as Q3 in the Phase A plan and deferred as out-of-scope for Phase A.
steps: Read spec/detailed/operators.allium for the RenameEquivalence rule and chaining semantics
Read src/operators.rs rename::apply to understand current non-chaining behavior
Write a failing test: define a rename op with two mappings where the second depends on the first's output (e.g., rename a→b then b→c; expect a→c, not a→b + b→c with b remaining)
Fix rename::apply to update the working file set after each mapping so subsequent mappings see the results of prior ones
Verify the test passes
Check for regressions in existing rename tests
Run: cargo nextest run
acceptance_criteria: rename::apply chains mappings: each mapping sees the result of previous mappings
Failing test demonstrates chaining behavior and passes after fix
No regressions in existing rename tests
Behavior matches RenameEquivalence spec invariant
All tests pass

notes: Phase A must be merged before starting Phase B implementation work
The failing E2E test (write-failing-e2e-test) is the load-bearing acceptance criterion — it proves the reported bug is fixed
Tasks 3-5 (refactor discovery, add resolve_repo_inline, make composite incremental) can potentially run in parallel if the implementation approach allows it
If any single task grows too large, use the plan-to-plan pattern from CLAUDE.md to break it into a sub-plan
The spec describes the final state. Implementation may deviate slightly in internal structure as long as observable behavior matches
Phase B changes include operator semantics (Q2 include-from-source-FS, Q3 rename chaining) that Phase A deferred. These may need to be implemented alongside the architectural change.
If design exploration reveals that Phase A's foundation needs changes, coordinate back through the plans rather than hacking Phase A retroactively
Items 13-15 added from Phase A code review follow-up (2026-04-13):
Item 13 (error-messages-sequential-context-hints): blocked on full-ci-and-commit because it requires the sequential pass to be complete before hints make sense
Item 14 (spec-phased-implementation-annotations): unblocked — pure documentation task, can be done anytime
Item 15 (rename-apply-chaining-fix): blocked on wire-sequential-pass because the chaining fix interacts with how rename ops execute in the sequential pass (Q3 deferred work from Phase A)

Item 3 (fix-load-local-fs-dotfiles) added 2026-04-14: load_local_fs blanket-skips all dotfiles, but common-repo's core purpose is managing dotfiles. The spec's MergeLocalFiles rule only skips specific dirs (.git, node_modules, etc.), not all hidden files. This blocks the Phase B bug scenario (self: block with local .pre-commit-config.yaml). Failing E2E tests in tests/cli_e2e_dotfiles.rs. Must be fixed before the repo-in-sequence architecture work (tasks 4+). All old priorities bumped by 1.
